stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Novelist Zora Neale Hurston was raised in Florida and graduated from Barnard College in New York City.
  • Several of Hurston's most acclaimed novels are set in Florida.
  • One such novel is Their Eyes Were Watching God (1937).
  • Hurston's novels often portray the lives of Black communities in the rural South.
stem: The student wants to identify the setting of Hurston's novel Their Eyes Were Watching God. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Hurston, author of Their Eyes Were Watching God, often portrayed Black communities in the rural South.
Hurston graduated from Barnard College in New York City and set some of her novels in Florida.
Their Eyes Were Watching God is set in Florida, where Hurston grew up.
Among Hurston's most acclaimed novels is Their Eyes Were Watching God (1937).

rationale: Choice C is the best answer because it directly identifies the setting of Their Eyes Were Watching God as Florida. Choice A is incorrect because it describes the kind of communities Hurston wrote about but doesn't identify where the novel takes place. Choice B is incorrect because it indicates that some of her novels are set in Florida but doesn't specify whether this particular novel is one of them. Choice D is incorrect because it names the novel without indicating its setting.
